notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms are svadharma (role-specific duty), api (additional argument marker), dharmya yuddha (battle conforming to dharma), and the 'na vidyate' (none exists) absolute. The engineering layer preserves all four. Svadharma is correctly anchored to role-specific accumulated context (the staff engineer's three-year ownership of the Postgres write-path) rather than collapsed to passion or general work-ethic; the 'api' is preserved both in the translation ('a second, independent argument') and acknowledged in the source pack disagreements section as Shankara reads ('even apart from the imperishable doctrine'). The dharma-yuddha-as-highest-good claim is honestly mapped to 'a confrontation with a hard but legitimate engineering problem... is the highest available good' — narrowly, with the role-alignment scope preserved. Critical preservation: the kṣatriya-specificity is not collapsed to 'follow your passion' — it is mapped to role-specific accumulated context, which is what svadharma actually names. The counter_example correctly invokes Chapter 5 / 18.66 as the right-renunciation corrective. PASS.
